title: asclepias tuberosa subsp. rolfsii
intro: Asclepias tuberosa subsp. rolfsii, also known as the Butterfly Milkweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asclepias tuberosa subsp. rolfsii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes.
